assumptions: Match engine: independent Poisson goals; each team's expected goals set by the Elo-rating gap (host nations +50 Elo).
Top teams' strength is fitted to the betting market's title odds (Kalshi via Covers, 13 Jun 2026); the rest of the field is set by world-football Elo (15 Jun 2026).

Second calibration input: any scheduled but unplayed group game that has a betting line in match_odds.json is priced directly off the market's 1X2 (home/draw/away) odds — the model solves the Poisson scoring rates that reproduce that line — instead of inferring the game from the Elo gap.
Completed results so far are locked in; all remaining group games and every knockout tie are simulated.
Knockout ties level after 90' are resolved by an Elo-logistic extra-time/penalty model.
Best-8 third-placed teams allocated to bracket slots using FIFA's exact Annex C table (495 combinations).
Probabilities from 40,000 Monte Carlo tournament simulations; sampling error on a 50% probability is about ±0.3%.
A few lower-tier teams' Elo values are estimated (flagged in data); they barely affect the knockout picture and refine as results post.

matchups.why: Bosnia & Herzegovina replaces Canada as South Korea's projected opponent — driven by Switzerland 1-1 Bosnia & Herzegovina (Group B). Still a wide-open slot (top pairing just 14%) — a lean, not a lock.
Switzerland replaces Bosnia & Herzegovina as USA's projected opponent — driven by Switzerland 1-1 Bosnia & Herzegovina (Group B). Still a wide-open slot (top pairing just 8.5%) — a lean, not a lock.

Bosnia & Herzegovina and Egypt replace Algeria and Canada as the projected pairing — driven by Switzerland 1-1 Bosnia & Herzegovina (Group B). Still a wide-open slot (top pairing just 4.6%) — a lean, not a lock.
Portugal replaces Colombia as Switzerland's projected opponent — driven by today's results, reshuffling the knockout seeding. Still a wide-open slot (top pairing just 5.5%) — a lean, not a lock.
England and France replace Argentina and Spain as the projected final — driven by today's results, reshuffling the knockout seeding. Still a wide-open slot (top final just 4.2%) — a lean, not a lock.

changes.summary: This rebuild ingested 3 new results (Czechia 1-0 South Africa (Group A); Switzerland 1-1 Bosnia & Herzegovina (Group B); Canada 1-1 Qatar (Group B)). 5 bracket boxes now show a different pairing — see each for what moved it.
